Abstract

We present a novel approach to neural machine translation that achieves state-of-the-art results on multiple benchmarks. Our method, TransFormer++, combines attention mechanisms with hierarchical encoding to capture both local and global context simultaneously.

Specifically, we introduce three key contributions:

  • A hierarchical attention mechanism that operates at both token and sentence levels
  • A novel positional encoding scheme that preserves relative distances across long sequences
  • An efficient training procedure using curriculum learning

Experiments on WMT14 English-German and English-French translation tasks demonstrate improvements of 2.1 BLEU and 1.8 BLEU respectively over the previous best results. Code and models are available at github.com/example/transforemer-plus.
